name: Cap Gros and the Muleta refuge — circular trailrunning in the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park
name_original: Cap Gros y el refugio de la Muleta — circular trailrunning por el Paraje Natural de la Serra de Tramuntana
region_intro: Jogging routes around Sóller are set within the UNESCO World Heritage Site of the Serra de Tramuntana mountains in Mallorca. The region features diverse landscapes, including dramatic limestone peaks, fertile valleys with orange and olive groves, and coastal areas. Historic stone paths and dry-stone walls crisscross the terrain, connecting villages and offering varied running surfaces. The area provides a mix of gentle valley runs and challenging mountain trails. Best jogging routes around Sóller The most popular jogging route is Cap Gros and the Muleta refuge — circular trailrunning in the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park, a difficult 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail that takes 45 minutes to complete. This route offers coastal views and traverses parts of the Serra de Tramuntana. Another top favourite among local runners is Port de Sóller Promenade – Cap Gros Lighthouse loop from Port de Sóller, a difficult 5.7 miles (9.2 km) path. This route follows the promenade and leads to the Cap Gros Lighthouse, providing expansive sea views. Local runners also love the Cap Gros Lighthouse – Cap Gros Lighthouse loop from Sóller, a difficult 6.9 miles (11.0 km) trail leading through coastal terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Jogging around Sóller is defined by Serra de Tramuntana mountains, fertile orange groves, and historic stone paths. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy valley runs to challenging trail running. The routes in Sóller are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 6,700 runners have used komoot to explore Sóller's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Sóller?
A: Sóller offers a wide variety of running and jogging routes, with nearly 100 different options recorded on komoot. These routes cater to all levels, from gentle valley runs to challenging mountain trails within the Serra de Tramuntana.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed pace?
A: Yes, Sóller has several easy jogging routes, with 7 options specifically categorized as easy. These often weave through the tranquil orange and olive groves of the Sóller Valley, providing a scenic and less strenuous experience. Look for routes that stick to the valley floor for a more relaxed run.

Q: What are some popular moderate running trails in the area?
A: For those seeking a moderate challenge, Sóller boasts 50 such routes. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14848282" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Sóller Promenade – Sóller Vintage Tram loop from Sóller&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 10 km and offers a mix of coastal and town scenery. Another excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19542905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Sóller Promenade – Port de Sóller Promenade loop from Port de Sóller&lt;/a&gt;, providing beautiful sea views.

Q: Can I find challenging trail running options around Sóller?
A: Absolutely. Sóller is renowned for its challenging trail running, with 39 difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000792777/cap-gros-and-the-muleta-refuge-circular-trailrunning-in-the-serra-de-tramuntana-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cap Gros and the Muleta refuge — circular trailrunning in the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, offering stunning coastal views and a good workout over 6 km. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/850688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Sóller Promenade – Cap Gros Lighthouse loop from Port de Sóller&lt;/a&gt;, which extends over 9 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Sóller?
A: Many of Sóller's running routes are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000792777/cap-gros-and-the-muleta-refuge-circular-trailrunning-in-the-serra-de-tramuntana-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cap Gros and the Muleta refuge — circular trailrunning in the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 6 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19541738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cap Gros Lighthouse – Cap Gros Lighthouse loop from Sóller&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 11 km coastal trail.

Q: What kind of scenery and landmarks can I expect to see while running in Sóller?
A: Running in Sóller offers incredibly diverse scenery. You'll pass through fragrant orange and lemon groves in the valley, traverse historic stone paths and dry-stone walls, and enjoy dramatic limestone peaks of the Serra de Tramuntana. Coastal routes provide breathtaking views of the Mediterranean Sea and the horseshoe-shaped bay of Port de Sóller. Notable landmarks include the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/94862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fornalutx Village&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/79577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biniaraix&lt;/a&gt;, and various viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/135809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Port de Sóller Bay and Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any mountain peaks or viewpoints accessible from running trails?
A: Yes, many running trails offer access to stunning mountain views and peaks. While some peaks require dedicated hiking, you can often find trails that provide panoramic vistas of the valley, coastline, and the Serra de Tramuntana. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4134048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Puig Major&lt;/a&gt; and the opportunity to see other summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/177268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de l'Ofre&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Sóller?
A: The running routes in Sóller are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an impressive average rating of 4.3 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 6,700 runners have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the stunning natural beauty, the variety of terrain, and the well-maintained historic paths that make for a unique running experience.

Q: Is Sóller a good destination for trail running?
A: Sóller is considered a prime destination for trail running, often called the 'hiking capital of Mallorca.' Its location within the Serra de Tramuntana provides a vast network of trails, from moderate paths through olive groves to challenging ascents on rocky ridges. The historic GR221, or 'Dry Stone Route,' also passes through the area, offering legendary trail running experiences.

Q: What are the best times of year to go running in Sóller?
A: Sóller offers excellent running conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly popular, with pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. Even in summer, early morning or late afternoon runs are enjoyable, especially on coastal paths or shaded valley trails. Winter provides cooler, crisp air, perfect for longer runs, though higher mountain trails might require checking weather conditions.

Q: Are there any routes that connect Sóller to Port de Sóller?
A: Yes, there are several scenic routes that connect Sóller to its port, Port de Sóller. These paths often provide beautiful coastal views and a pleasant change of scenery from the valley. The promenade in Port de Sóller itself is also suitable for a relaxed jog.

Q: What kind of natural features define the running landscape in Sóller?
A: The running landscape in Sóller is defined by its dramatic natural features. You'll encounter the towering limestone peaks of the Serra de Tramuntana, the lush, fragrant orange and lemon groves of the Sóller Valley, and the sparkling Mediterranean coastline. Historic dry-stone walls and ancient cobbled paths are also prominent, adding a unique character to the trails.
tips: Great view of the harbor of Port de Sóller, the Torre Picada and the Puig de Bàlitx.

As a flatland Hamburg resident, I had some difficulty with the climb. I was able to find the dog, but then I couldn't find the path and turned back. I would also describe the path as difficult and recommend very good footwear, no sneakers.
Typical Malle-Karrenweg - big stones, steps, places to push and wet are not recommended. But: through beautiful area with worthwhile sightseeing graduation at the lighthouse
